creator: chris.dukes.aix@gmail.com
text:
I meant for this to go to the bug report when I replied to dinoex.
----
It looks like gnu89 is being defined /usr/ports/Mk's
bsd.gnome.mk:813:USE_CSTD=    gnu89

That then leaks to CFLAGS at
bsd.port.mk:2222
bsd.gnome.mk:811:# Set USE_CSTD for all ports that depend on glib12
bsd.gnome.mk:813:USE_CSTD=    gnu89
bsd.port.mk:321:# USE_CSTD        - Override the default C language 
standard (gnu89, gnu99)
bsd.port.mk:2221:.if defined(USE_CSTD)
bsd.port.mk:2222:CFLAGS:=    ${CFLAGS:N-std=*} -std=${USE_CSTD}

At which point the following from configure infects things with the bad 
flag
configure:21588:                        LUA_INCLUDES="$LUA_CFLAGS 
"`$PKGCONFIG --cflags lua$l`
configure:21594:                   LUA_INCLUDES="$LUA_CFLAGS 
"`$PKGCONFIG --cflags lualib$l`
configure.ac:881:                        LUA_INCLUDES="$LUA_CFLAGS 
"`$PKGCONFIG --cflags lua$l`
configure.ac:887:                   LUA_INCLUDES="$LUA_CFLAGS 
"`$PKGCONFIG --cflags lualib$l`

A patch against configure.ac and autoconf/automake invocation within the 
directory should do the right thing, but I don't remember the right way 
to do that.

It looks to be equally broken on Ubuntu 12.10, so I suspect an email to 
the upstream maintainer is in order.
----
The proper invocation of $PKGCONFIG for this purpose would be 
--clfags-only-I which should prevent the injection of '-std=gnu89' to 
clang++.
